content.boundary: Applies to funding allocation formulas, curriculum design, and retention/promotion policy for comprehensive-track and feeder schools; explicitly excludes any change to the 6/8-year entry age, admission criteria, or capacity levels.
content.change_lever: Redirection and targeting of public education finance toward disadvantaged/feeder schools (TEIP-style targeted funding), combined with curriculum-clarity reforms and retention-reduction measures, without altering the tracking age or admission architecture.
content.name: Non-structural compensatory equity package (targeted funding, curriculum clarity, retention reduction) with selection age unchanged
content.system_problem: Hungary's low overall education spending share (4.1% of government expenditure) and below-average instructional time interact with a capitation funding model that removes both funding and positive peer effects from feeder schools when high-performing students depart for selective tracks, compounding disadvantage in non-selective schools without any compensatory mechanism.
